info.topictitle: A3.4.13 Circuit diagram symbols and components
info.parenttitle: A3.4 Introduction to electronic systems (HL only)

markdown:
### Circuit Diagrams


<definition term="Circuit Diagram">**Circuit diagrams** are **visual representations** of **electronic circuits**, using **symbols for components** and **lines to show connections**.</definition>

1. **Clarity**: Simplifies complex circuits for easy understanding.
2. **Standardisation**: Uses universal symbols for global comprehension.
3. **Efficiency**: Allows quick identification of components and their functions.

<callout type="analogy">**Circuit diagrams** are like blueprints for electronic systems, providing a clear and concise way to visualize how components are connected and interact.</callout>

<callout type="note">#### IEC vs ANSI


* Most of the circuit diagrams in this course use **IEC (International Electrotechnical Commission)** symbols, which are the **international standard**. 
* These are widely used in Europe and around the world.
* You might occasionally see **ANSI** (American National Standards Institute) symbols, especially in resources from the **United States**.</callout>

### Key Components and Their Symbols


#### Fixed Resistor


<definition term="Resistor">An electronic component that **limits or controls the flow of electric current** in a circuit.</definition>

![Two standard symbols for a fixed resistor: IEC (left) and ANSI (right).](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/d0e236b29824193b0d24e57780f58de554a84a6b-504x384.png)

#### Variable Resistor


<definition term="Variable resistors">**Variable resistors** are components that allow you to adjust the resistance in a circuit.</definition>

![Standard IEC symbols for a variable resistor IEC](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/a33ff014e68e2c903576f62f1d1b4eab6c035450-3840x1920.png)

#### Capacitors


<definition term="Capacitor">An electronic component that **stores and releases electrical energy** by holding an **electric charge** between two conductive plates.</definition>

![Standard IEC symbols for a Non-Polarized and Polarized Capacitor](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/610209d2b2b25d8e53cce6e120e5568748d130ad-1000x800.png)

#### Switches


<definition term="Switch">An electrical component that is used to **connect or disconnect the flow of electricity** in a circuit, allowing control over whether the circuit is **on or off**.</definition>

![Standard IEC symbol for a Single-Pole Single-Throw (SPST) switch](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/cc80003ed77fc511dea52f89ffdc26e2f9de8c07-1024x324.png)

![Standard IEC symbol for a Single-Pole Double-Throw (SPDT) switch](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/a2222c87d1811ecd22394b200ff56c02e2e5e0fb-324x155.png)

#### Relays


<definition term="Relay">An **electronic switch** that is **activated by a current or signal** to **open or close another circuit**, allowing a **low-power signal to control a high-power circuit**.</definition>

![Standard IEC symbol for a relay](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/9df3e10a65c32370e9ce1db8b315c6cdf7e480a6-1280x832.png)

#### Diodes


<definition term="Diode">An electronic component that **allows current to flow in only one direction**, acting like a **one-way valve** in a circuit.</definition>

![Standard IEC symbols for a diode](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/38c799a5bda76a47e18abd5325330de39877d483-1495x717.png)

#### Transistors


<definition term="Transistor">A **semiconductor device** with **three connections** that can **regulate current or voltage** and is commonly used for **switching or amplifying signals** in electronic circuits.</definition>

![Two IEC standard symbols for a transistor: NPN (left) and PNP (right)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/ad09489660b1af2704385d08209bd09fe482e8ef-483x280.png)

#### Operational Amplifiers (Op-Amps)


<definition term="Operational amplifier (op-amp)">An electronic device that **amplifies weak signals**.</definition>

![Standard IEC symbols for an op-amp](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/c833ebaf10ec894031d67ce86becb1edef9bd491-1024x857.png)

#### Light Emitting Diode


![Standard IEC symbol for an Light Emitting Diode (LED)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/0f06058699bb8dcfdd6b0ef1ebc9410840ed3192-1864x894.png)

#### Light Dependent Resistor (LDR)


![Standard IEC symbol for an Light Dependent Resistor (LDR)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/ce9dbad140160a6ebe5db11cf54416e0797257c4-600x200.png)

#### Microphone


![Standard IEC symbol for a Microphone](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/e40d6b31f1df801bb3b37962a7a9335ca39c5bc4-1927x2114.png)

#### Speaker


![Standard IEC symbol for a Speaker](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/2115681e05a9eb13f1ffd9c9b3432e2295f90787-200x200.png)

#### Motor


![Standard IEC symbol for a Motor](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/8572e504b966f7e84d722f2d7e41a3e874e0263c-1024x1024.png)

#### Fuse


![Standard IEC symbol for a Fuse](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/0e219cd89cd1812fa87202ef3589a96625207642-980x980.png)

#### Battery


![Standard IEC symbol for a Battery](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/8086def3fcfe17b1506f5df1b742d4c1528ea07b-1880x902.png)

#### Voltmeter


![Standard IEC symbol for a Voltmeter](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/4f728932108122e25b75986f9ea5305f830bdf95-1286x617.png)

#### Ammeter


![Standard IEC symbol for a Ammeter](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/d4e7c8b6d260cd84f20f00de2f2123154fd38d42-1516x727.png)
